summary: The CI job produced 1143 errors across numerous compiletest UI, codegen, and run-make tests. The dominant failures appear to be tests being unexpectedly 'ignored' on the target platform (likely a wasm/exceptions/asm target), plus git submodule locking errors during setup.
root_cause: A large number of compiletest UI and codegen tests are reporting 'ignored' as their outcome when the test runner expected them to run (or pass), indicating the target platform configuration (likely wasm with exceptions) causes widespread test skipping that is treated as a failure. Additionally, git submodule config locking errors suggest environment setup issues.

evidence.reason: Multiple high-frequency error entries show tests reporting 'ignored' status, suggesting the target platform causes mass test skipping which is being counted as failures

evidence.quote: error: could not lock config file .git/modules/library/backtrace/con...
evidence.reason: Git submodule locking errors (×12) indicate environment/setup issues that may have disrupted the build or test environment

evidence.quote: warning: Could not unset core.worktree setting in submodule 'src/doc...
evidence.reason: Submodule configuration warnings corroborate git environment setup problems alongside the locking errors
The sheer volume of errors (1143 total, 221 unique) across ui, codegen, and run-make test suites suggests a systemic platform/target mismatch rather than individual code bugs

evidence.quote: test [ui] tests/ui/asm/cfg-parse-error.rs ... ignored, ignored on ta...
evidence.reason: The partial text 'ignored on ta...' strongly suggests 'ignored on target' — confirming the wasm/exceptions target is causing tests to be skipped en masse

suggested_fix: Investigate why tests are being marked 'ignored on target' for the wasm-exceptions target — check whether the target triple used in this CI job matches what the tests expect, and resolve the git submodule locking errors by ensuring no concurrent git operations are running during setup (the 'could not lock config file .git/modules/library/backtrace/config' error suggests a race or stale lock).
